name: pravo-ritorika
description: Classical rhetoric for legal positions on Russian legal practice: status theory (choosing the line of dispute — fact, definition, evaluation, procedure), dispositio (argument order and text structure), the logos/ethos/pathos balance, audience models (court, notary, state body, negotiation counterparty, client), and steelman stress-testing. Modes: stress-test, status, structure, adaptation. The paired skill to pravo-logika: logic checks whether the conclusion follows, rhetoric checks whether the addressee will accept it. Validity is out of scope — logical defects are flagged to pravo-logika; only honest argumentation is strengthened. Statutes and case law are flagged for human verification, never asserted from memory.
